title: Barlaea calcarata
taxonomy: Barlaea calcarata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Orchideae. The genus Barlaea is a small group of terrestrial orchids, native to North America. Barlaea calcarata specifically is a rare orchid found in the southwestern United States. More research is needed to determine its full classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Barlaea calcarata is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine, primarily in Eastern Asia. It is valued for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The plant's extracts have been used to treat a variety of conditions, including fever and respiratory infections. Research has also suggested that Barlaea calcarata may have potential antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Further studies are needed to fully explore the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Barlaea calcarata, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which can aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Barlaea calcarata include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Barlaea calcarata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
